config-patch knowledge gap converged after 3 cycles without recurrence. ct_009 (project check) noted HTTP 400 but kept open — likely provider transient.
Marked ct_002 as regressed. This is the 2nd fix attempt. verify_after extended to evt_109.

ct_002 3rd REGRESSION: root-caused to thinking:'off' parameter unique to weather cron — all other sonnet-4-6 jobs (11-16s) succeed while weather (300-600s) times out with FailoverError: LLM request timed out. Fix: thinking:off→low, web_fetch→web_search, timeout 600→120s. Manual test triggered.
Marked ct_002 as regressed (3rd time). verify_after extended to evt_110. Added detailed fix history to notes.
Project check HTTP 400 at 06:00 was provider transient (gptclub-openai). Job config correct. 12:00 run pending.

4th fix: delete+recreate job without thinking field. Previous 3 fixes failed because cron patch cannot remove fields and any thinking value (off/low/none) causes LLM hang. New clean job abe78dcc. Also deleted orphan f542a607.
New tracker for DeepRouter timeout — 'xhigh' thinking applied despite no config, suggests global agent default interference.
Project check converged after 3 successful runs. ct_002 updated as 4th fix attempt.

CORRECTED ROOT CAUSE: All 3 failing crons (weather/deeprouter/archiver) share identical FailoverError: LLM request timed out in 18:49-20:07 CST window. This is gptclub-claude PROVIDER intermittent timeout, NOT thinking field contamination (fixes #3-4 chased wrong cause). Other gptclub-claude jobs succeeded (14-23s) in overlapping windows, proving it's transient not systemic.
Removed web_search step for lunar dates to reduce LLM round-trips from 4+ to 3, shrinking the execution window where a provider timeout can hit.
New tracker for thread archiver first-time timeout. Same provider wave as ct_002/ct_010.

Grounded definitive lane congestion diagnostic guide from Mar 10 8-hour investigation. Documents root cause (zombie tasks on high-traffic channel lanes), 6-fix history, diagnostic procedure (fresh sessionKey test), and prevention rules.

MAJOR DIAGNOSIS CORRECTION: ct_002 root cause updated from 'provider timeout' (evt_109) to 'session lane congestion' (proven by 23:13 experiment). ct_010 updated similarly. ct_011 converged (self-recovered without intervention).

Verified weather (17b6b649) and DeepRouter (c199b7ea) share sessionKey 1480851680445595769 but schedules don't overlap (07:00 vs 09:00-17:00). No action needed. Creation-time forced run collision likely caused DeepRouter's initial timeout.

actions.target: DeepRouter余额监控 + 早安天气播报
actions.summary: CRITICAL: Both jobs shared sessionKey 1480851680445595769 — weather timeout at 07:37 created zombie blocking DeepRouter at 09:00 (cascade). evt_110 audit said no action needed (wrong — didnt account for zombie persistence). Fix: patched both to unique sessionKeys (agent:main:cron:deeprouter-balance, agent:main:cron:weather-morning).
Added NEVER-SHARE-SESSIONKEY rule: zombie tasks persist indefinitely, even non-overlapping schedules cause cascade. Each cron job MUST have unique sessionKey.
Both updated with shared-sessionKey cascade as compounding root cause. verify_after extended to evt_114. Fix #7 for weather, #2 for DeepRouter.

Fix #8: Delete+recreate (remove stale lightContext:true), model switch astralor/claude-sonnet-4-6 → kimi-coding/k2p5. New job 5ce0c2fd with unique sessionKey agent:main:cron:weather-morning. Evidence: 8 consecutive failures across 3 job IDs on Astralor, zero LLM API calls in logs, while subagent succeeds and other Sonnet jobs work fine. Bypassing model-route-specific failure.
Documented pi-embedded maxConcurrent patch fragility (overwritten by upgrades). Updated cron-lane-congestion.md with model-route-specific failure pattern and expanded key lessons.
ct_002 updated with fix #8 (model switch), verify at evt_115. ct_010 positive trend (multiple ok runs). New ct_012 for pi-embedded patch fragility.

DEFINITIVE ROOT CAUSE CORRECTION: evt_112 said 'model-route-specific failure' and switched to kimi-coding. WRONG — weather cron is now back on astralor/claude-sonnet-4-6 at 43s success after pi-embedded maxConcurrent patch. The 'zero LLM API calls' was maxConcurrent=1 blocking, not provider routing. Fix #9 (patch) is the true fix. Updated tracker and cron-lane-congestion.md.

Replaced 'model-route-specific failure' section with 'definitive fix' section. Corrected diagnostic priority: check maxConcurrent patch FIRST, lane SECOND, provider THIRD. Added 'never attribute to model routing what infrastructure explains'.

CONVERGED: DeepRouter余额监控 stable since sessionKey separation (evt_111). lastDurationMs=8193, consecutiveErrors=0 across 3+ cycles. Root cause eliminated.

Grounded PR triple-location text check guardrail from blog PR#19 human-intervention (繁→简 fix missed PR title/body). Added pre-submit checklist and reviewer selection strategy from PR #41903 compliance audit.

ct_002: Weather cron healthy (43s, 0 errors), 1 more cycle to converge. ct_012: Patch intact, no upgrade, PR #41903 still OPEN with 0 human reviews after 2 days — extended to evt_118. New ct_013 for PR multi-location text fix.

All 7 jobs migrated to gptclub-openai/gpt-5.4 running healthy (14-83s, 0 consecutive errors). astralor usage reduced from ~110 to ~8 daily calls. No degradation observed in output quality or reliability.

CONVERGED: Weather cron saga closed after 11 cycles and 9 fix attempts. Longest tracker in PCEC v3 history. Definitive fix (pi-embedded maxConcurrent patch) held stable for 4+ cycles at 43s completion, 0 errors. Key lesson: infrastructure root causes masked by application symptoms — check maxConcurrent FIRST.

Full code audit of v0.2.0 upgrade (footnote processing, bold regex fix, dark mode styles). Code clean and well-structured. PR #41903 status update: 48+ hours since @vincentkoc ping, 0 human reviews. Risk of burial in 5458-PR queue. Escalation plan: second ping or try steipete by evt_118.

Grounded executable blog writing self-check rules (grep commands) from 03-11 daily log. Previous rules in MEMORY.md were principle-level only ('don't use oral style') — failed to prevent recurrence because they weren't mechanically checkable. New reference file provides runnable grep patterns for: dramatic short sentences, oral-style transitions, self-referential passages, footnote uniqueness.

Updated MEMORY.md checklist to point to reference file and replaced 'read aloud' heuristic with 'PR triple-location check'. The key insight: rules that say 'don't do X' fail; rules that say 'run grep for X' succeed.

ct_012: PR #41903 still OPEN after 72h, 0 human reviews, patch verified intact. ct_013: PR workflow checklist grounded, awaiting next PR to verify. New ct_014: blog writing pattern recurrence — executable grep checklist created to replace principle-level rules.

No quality or reliability degradation. Migration confirmed healthy. Durations: 28-100s range, consistent with pre-migration baselines.

PR #41903 status at ~96h: still 0 human reviews. Patch verified intact (both files maxConcurrent:10). OpenClaw still 2026.3.8, no upgrade risk. @vincentkoc pinged 48h ago, no response. Extended verify_after to evt_121. No further escalation action.

OpenLinkOS dev-engineer cron (1dbe496f) hit transient gptclub-openai API error. consecutiveErrors=1, single occurrence. Previous run was ok. Not worth a tracker entry — will self-recover. If reaches 3+ consecutive, investigate gptclub-openai stability with gpt-5.4.
Increased timeoutSeconds 300→600. Last run consumed 93% of timeout (279s/300s) after migration to gpt-5.4 — one slightly slower run would have caused failure. Preemptive fix before first timeout.

Added explicit timeoutSeconds:600 to newly created AI Feed cron. First run was 341s with no timeout set — good hygiene to bound all jobs. Also verified: 3 open convergence trackers (ct_012/013/014) unchanged, no recurrence, no regression.

Full audit of new feed-collector skill (launched today). Findings: (1) Model not aligned with 03-11 migration — uses astralor/sonnet at 371s×6/day vs target of minimizing astralor usage, tracked as ct_015. (2) Build verification marked optional despite AstroPaper template gotchas discovered today (deleted resource references break Cloudflare build). (3) Dedup working correctly — 33 seen entries for 34 articles, URL-based matching sufficient for current sources. (4) No partial-failure recovery guidance — if cron fails mid-collection, next run re-scrapes everything. (5) Overall: well-structured for v1.0.0, no urgent fixes needed.
New tracker for feed cron model alignment gap. Created before migration decision, never reviewed. Not changing unilaterally — quality tradeoff is human decision.
3 open items unchanged. ct_012: PR #41903 ~100h, 0 human reviews, no action. ct_013: awaiting next PR. ct_014: awaiting next blog post. No regressions, no convergence.

Grounded 'PR supersession' lesson from PR #41903 experience. vincentkoc's maintainer PR #43683 supersedes our community PR. Documented pattern: maintainer PRs always win, community PR value is in issue discovery + diagnosis, don't compete — close gracefully when superseded.

Created diagnostic patterns reference from today's WebSocket handshake timeout incident. Two patterns grounded: (1) HTTP probe OK ≠ WebSocket OK — gateway status uses different code path from CLI. (2) Config value not taking effect — bundler state isolation. Both sourced from real production incidents with specific root causes.

Version check cron (21e8f123) has consecutiveErrors=1 — GPT-5.4 transient API error. One-off, monitoring. All other 10 crons healthy (consecutiveErrors=0). v2026.3.12 WebSocket bug (#44714) affects CLI only, not cron/internal operations.

Added 'Tell → Show → Explain' structural framework to blog checklist. Discovered from today's concurrency lanes article — ChatGPT review confirmed this structure works better than exploratory narrative for engineering blogs. Includes anti-patterns (elevator metaphor, over-expanded cross-validation) and positive patterns (conclusion-first, diagram-first, workload isolation terminology). Pre-flight step 4 now requires explicit structure choice.

Full green: 11/11 crons lastStatus=ok, consecutiveErrors=0. v2026.3.12 stable for 5+ hours. WebSocket CLI bug (#44714) confirmed — affects CLI only, not operations. Version check cron recovered from evt_125 transient error (consecutiveErrors back to 0).

Fixed incorrect SKILL.md path in cron prompt: ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/feed-collector/SKILL.md (nonexistent) → /data/code/github.com/best/openclaw-skills/feed-collector/SKILL.md (correct extraDirs path). Agent was working despite wrong path because prompt included enough inline instructions, but this ensures the agent can read the full SKILL.md with YAML safety rules, validation steps, and sourceType enum.

gptclub-claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 transient error again (consecutiveErrors=1). 2nd occurrence in 3 cycles (evt_125, evt_127). Error: generic OpenAI 5xx. Only 1 cron job uses this provider. Grounded WeChat HTML rendering rules from v0.5.0 debugging marathon: verified which tags get styles preserved (<section>, <span>) vs forcibly overridden (<pre>/<code>, <strong>, <a>), and documented all workarounds (section nesting for code, span for bold, p+bullet for lists). 4+ rounds of human-driven fixes distilled into reusable reference.

Added fact-verification guardrail: when citing default values, must verify with source code grep — not from memory or own config. Triggered by concurrency lanes blog incorrectly stating cron default=5 (our config) when source code says ??1. Added rule to distinguish 'our config' from 'system defaults'.

Grounded Codex sandbox network limitation: no internet access causes pnpm install/vitest to timeout. Added workaround (external verification or CC subagent review) and operational rule. Source: 2026-03-13 handshake fix session where Codex self-review failed.

Created work item for unpushed handshake timeout fix. Branch fix/loopback-handshake-timeout has 2 commits (fix + IPv6 test), CC review passed as PR-ready, but not pushed. Risk of being lost across sessions. Blocked on human confirmation.

Full audit post-release. SKILL.md comprehensive (8 gotchas documented with version tags). README tables in sync. Script 396 lines, well-structured. Minor: ul/ol conversion logic duplicated, nested lists unhandled (theoretical, no production signal). Skill is mature. No fix needed.

Grounded cron dual-delivery prevention rules: never use announce + message tool simultaneously, all prompt branches must have message tool calls, K2.5 model suitability guide. Source: 2026-03-14 daily log where human identified 6 jobs with duplicate output.

Grounded CommandLane architecture from 2026-03-13 source code analysis: 3 independent lanes (Main/Subagent/Cron), dual-layer queue (session→global), cron default=1 not 5, key code locations. Prevents re-derivation of this knowledge in future sessions.
ct_017 (Codex sandbox network): open, 1 cycle since creation. ct_018 (cron dual-delivery): NEW, open. wi_003 (handshake PR): still open, awaiting human confirmation. No regressions.
